title: CrackArmor Campaign Investigation
turns: what is the CrackArmor campaign and should i be worried about it
which CVEs are associated with CrackArmor
are we exposed to any of those CVEs in our environment
what threat actors are behind this campaign
give me a summary i can send to the CISO

title: Iran APT33 Energy Sector Targeting
turns: i'm seeing news about APT33 targeting energy sector companies, are we at risk
what CVEs is APT33 actively exploiting right now
do we have any of those in our environment
any EDR or endpoint events related to this campaign
which assets are most exposed and how do we protect them

title: Volt Typhoon / Salt Typhoon Chinese Infrastructure Targeting
turns: tell me about Volt Typhoon and Salt Typhoon — are we a target
what techniques and CVEs are these groups using
do we run any of the targeted network infrastructure — routers, VPNs, firewalls
what's our exposure look like if they came after us
what hardening steps should we prioritize given this threat

title: RansomHub Healthcare Ransomware Triage
turns: RansomHub is hitting healthcare organizations — what's our ransomware exposure look like
which CVEs are they using to get initial access
how many of our assets are vulnerable to those specific CVEs
what's our patch status on the RansomHub-associated vulnerabilities
walk me through the remediation priorities

title: Ivanti Connect Secure Zero-Day Chain Incident Response
turns: CVE-2024-21887 and CVE-2023-46805 are being chained in attacks on Ivanti Connect Secure — do we run Ivanti
how many Ivanti assets do we have and what versions are they running
are both CVEs present on the same assets — that's the dangerous combo
any EDR or FIM events on those Ivanti hosts that look suspicious
what's the remediation path and what mitigations can we apply right now while we wait for patches
draft me an incident response summary for this

title: FortiManager FortiJump CVE-2024-47575 Exposure
turns: there's a critical zero-day in FortiManager called FortiJump — CVE-2024-47575 — do we run FortiManager
what versions do we have and are they in the vulnerable range
how many assets are affected and where are they sitting in the network
what mitigations has Fortinet published while we wait for the patch
what's the risk if we can't patch immediately — is this being actively exploited

title: XZ Utils Supply Chain Attack CVE-2024-3094
turns: the XZ Utils supply chain backdoor CVE-2024-3094 is huge news — check our Linux fleet
which Linux distributions and versions are affected
how many of our Linux assets have the compromised xz-utils package
are any of those systems internet-facing or running sshd
what's the remediation — downgrade or patch, and what's the fastest path

title: Lazarus Group Financial Services Targeting
turns: Lazarus Group is reportedly targeting financial services firms — check our APT exposure
what CVEs and techniques is Lazarus currently using
do we have active vulnerabilities that match their known TTPs
any threat indicators or EDR activity that might suggest reconnaissance against us
what's our overall risk posture against Lazarus and what should we do about it

title: CISA KEV Deadline Review
turns: CISA KEV deadlines are coming up — which known exploited vulnerabilities do we still have open
how many of those are past their remediation deadline
which assets still have open KEV findings
what's blocking remediation on the overdue ones
give me a prioritized list to work through this week

title: Zero-Day Firewall Triage
turns: we just got alerted about a new zero-day on firewall appliances — help me triage this
what firewall vendors and products do we have in the environment
are any of those exposed to the internet directly
what do we know about exploitation — is there a working exploit in the wild
what can we do right now before a patch is available

title: Monday Morning Security Standup
turns: good morning, i need to run the weekly standup — what happened over the weekend
anything new that came in from threat intel
what are the top priorities for the team this week
which items are overdue from last week
give me a quick briefing i can kick off the standup with

title: Sprint Planning — Patch Prioritization
turns: sprint planning today — help me figure out what to patch this sprint
which vulns give us the best risk reduction per effort
focus on the ones with active exploits in the wild
are there any compensating mitigations we can apply for things we can't patch this sprint
give me a ranked list of 10 vulnerabilities to commit to this sprint

title: PCI Audit Preparation
turns: PCI audit is in 3 weeks — where do we stand on compliance
which controls are we failing right now
how many systems are failing those controls
do we have any exceptions that auditors are going to scrutinize
what's our remediation plan for the gaps and can we close them in time

title: Board Report Preparation
turns: board meeting next week — i need to put together the security section, help me gather the data
what's our overall risk score and trend over the past quarter
what were the top 3 security events or risks this quarter
what's the ROI story on the security investments we've made
draft me an executive summary i can put in the board deck

title: Container Image Security Review
turns: i need to do a container security review — what's the state of our images
which images have critical vulnerabilities
which of those vulnerable images are actually running in production right now
what's the worst container in prod from a risk perspective
what's the remediation plan — rebuild from scratch or patch in place

title: Multi-Cloud Security Posture Review
turns: i need a full cloud security posture review across all our cloud providers
which provider has the worst posture right now
break down the AWS findings by account — which accounts are failing the most
what are the highest severity misconfigurations across all providers
which findings are internet-exposed and need immediate attention

title: Certificate Rotation Planning
turns: i need to plan certificate rotations — what certs are expiring in the next 90 days
any already expired that we missed
are there any weak cipher suites or deprecated TLS versions we should fix at the same time
which systems are highest priority — customer-facing, internal, etc
give me a rotation schedule i can bring to the team

title: EOL System Remediation Planning
turns: i need to tackle our EOL system problem — what are we dealing with
which EOL systems are internet-facing or in a high-risk zone
what's the actual vulnerability exposure on those EOL systems
help me prioritize which to upgrade or decommission first
what does a realistic 90-day remediation plan look like

title: Vulnerability Exception Review
turns: time for our quarterly exception review — show me what we have approved
which exceptions are expiring in the next 30 days
any of those excepted vulnerabilities now showing active exploitation in the wild
which exceptions do you think we should reconsider based on current threat intel
help me draft justifications for the ones we want to renew

title: Scanner Fleet Health Check
turns: i need to check our scanner fleet health — how are they doing
which scanners are offline or not responding
when did those last successfully complete a scan
how much of our environment has stale scan data because of this
what do we need to do to get full coverage back

title: Full Risk Assessment to Remediation Pipeline
turns: give me a full risk assessment — where do we stand overall
what's the single highest-risk CVE in our environment right now, tell me everything about it
how many assets are affected and what business systems are at stake
what does remediation look like for that CVE — patch, workaround, timeline
does remediating that CVE also help with any compliance gaps we have

title: CVE Incident Response Workflow
turns: CVE-2024-6387 regreSSHion just dropped — are we affected
pull up all the assets running vulnerable OpenSSH versions
any endpoint detection events on those hosts in the last 24 hours
which assets should we prioritize for emergency patching
what's the full incident response action plan

title: Compliance-Driven Remediation Workflow
turns: our SOC2 audit found gaps — help me figure out what to fix first
which failing controls have the most systems out of compliance
what patches or config changes would close the most compliance gaps
can we use the eliminate/mitigation program to address any of these without patching
once we remediate, how do we verify the controls are passing again

title: Cloud to Container Risk Chain
turns: i want to map our full cloud risk — start with cloud posture across all accounts
now show me the container images running in the worst cloud accounts
any web applications hosted in those same cloud environments with vulnerabilities
are there any certificate issues on the web apps in those cloud accounts
give me the full risk chain from cloud misconfiguration to container to app to cert

title: Threat Intelligence to Action Workflow
turns: tell me about BlackCat/ALPHV ransomware group — are they relevant to us
check our exposure to the CVEs BlackCat is actively exploiting
based on our exposure, prioritize which patches we need to deploy urgently
does remediating these patches help with our CIS benchmark compliance
summarize the threat, our exposure, and the action plan

title: QID Deep-Dive Investigation Workflow
turns: i keep seeing QID 375560 show up in reports — what is it exactly
what's the QDS score on that and why is it ranked so high
show me all the assets that have that QID open right now
what's the fix and is it a simple patch or is there more to it
can we bulk-remediate this or does each asset need to be handled individually

title: Tag-Based Risk Reduction Workflow
turns: i want to understand risk by asset tag — which of our tagged groups is in the worst shape
drill into the Production tag — what does the vulnerability landscape look like there
what are the top 5 vulns driving risk in that production group
what would our TruRisk score look like if we patched just those top 5
help me build a remediation plan just for the production tag

title: Multi-CVE Triage and Prioritization
turns: i have three CVEs i need to triage: CVE-2024-38063, CVE-2024-43461, and CVE-2024-38217 — give me the rundown on each
which of the three is the highest severity and most actively exploited
how many assets do we have exposed to each one
given our exposure, which should we patch first and what's the order
do any of these have compensating mitigations i can apply while the patches roll out

title: Weekly Security Review
turns: friday afternoon — let's do the weekly security review, what's the headline
what new vulnerabilities came in this week that we need to care about
anything from threat intel that changes our priorities
what actions do i need to assign to the team for next week
generate me a weekly summary i can email to leadership

title: Post-Incident Cleanup Workflow
turns: we just contained an incident on our web tier — help me understand what happened from a vulnerability standpoint
which CVEs on those web servers could have been the initial access vector
how many other systems in our environment have the same vulnerabilities — what's the blast radius
what's the full remediation plan to make sure this can't happen again
how do we verify we're clean and not still compromised on any other systems
